baselines.absence_evidence: No qualifying end-to-end selected-profile product cohort exists as of the baseline date.
baselines.cohort: At least five first-time internal operators using only supported selected-profile product surfaces.
baselines.method: Timestamp first eligible sign-in, first valid preview, genesis approval, effect review, terminal inspection, and replay; retain typed blockers.

baselines.absence_evidence: No terminal selected-profile runtime and fault cohort exists as of the baseline date.
baselines.cohort: Thirty selected-profile runs, including at least ten declared crash, restart, stale-authority, or ambiguous-effect injections.
baselines.method: Replay owner records and exported evidence; independently reproduce verification and score every terminal, refused, recovered, or ambiguous effect.

baselines.absence_evidence: No invoice-reconciled selected-profile run cohort exists as of the baseline date.
baselines.cohort: Thirty successful selected-profile runs with route-attempt, tool, runtime, storage, and supplier-attributable measurements.
baselines.method: Reconcile measured internal cost to each accepted run; report p50, p90, fallback amplification, and unattributed cost without treating accounting as cash movement.

baselines.absence_evidence: No qualifying first-time-operator comprehension cohort exists as of the baseline date.
baselines.cohort: At least five first-time internal operators with no implementation-guide access during the selected journey.
baselines.method: Record exposed architecture terms, correct blocker interpretation, unsupported-success attempts, and a post-task comprehension check.

blocker_ledger.summary: The selected package-to-genesis System journey has no terminal live owner path.
Live partial objects are not bound into the selected immutable profile, complete network-blocked local journey, and conditional attach, use, inspect, detach, and continuation overlay.
Deployment-local authentication and locally permitted exact-effect authority remain separate in canon but are not terminal through the selected product and final-invoker path.
The optional connected provider-neutral sign-in, passkey step-up, context-bound portable grant, and final-invoker revalidation path is not terminal.
The file.apply_patch leaf exists, but selected GoalRun, authority, revocation, IFC, fence, receipt, and recovery ordering do not.

No one selected inspection/export reconstructs diff, tests, evidence, receipts, root, costs, route, learning eligibility, restart, backup, restore, offline verification, and managed binding, data-view, lease, custody, usage, and charge evidence.

Backup, restore preview, restore application, clean-target reconstruction, and offline verification exist only as adjacent routes; they are not bound into one selected-profile proof with authority, currentness, checkpoint equivalence, valid successor-root lineage, and typed refusal evidence.

The optional managed overlay has no one selected route set that explicitly attaches a named service, admits one use, and reconstructs its binding, data-view, lease, RuntimeAssignment, custody, usage, charge, and receipt evidence.
The optional managed overlay has no one selected revoke or detach path proving that locally satisfied dependencies continue without hidden managed authority, custody, execution, or billing.
Managed revoke or detach with post-detach continuation and selected package/profile proposal, activation, rollback or recall, and System retirement are not terminal.
wallet.network method checks include legacy compatibility and are not estate-wide production PEP closure.
Native blockchain methods are inventoried, but generic authority, final-leaf ordering, receipts, and recovery remain method-specific and partial.
Replica writer-epoch fencing is real, but the externally reachable AGRS2 listener has no authenticated peer authority, effect policy, revocation, or IFC gate.
OAuth state and PKCE checks precede local token persistence, but owner authority, hardened secret evidence, idempotency, and recovery are not terminal.
The editor proxy gates new connections with an active lease but cannot classify, fence, receipt, or recover arbitrary forwarded byte effects.
A static candidate or handler boundary is recorded without a terminal pre-effect order proof.
The declared surface is unmounted, dynamic, or otherwise unavailable.
Development replay and compatibility behavior cannot supply owner truth or authority.
JavaScript application and adapter crossings are protocol clients, never authority or durable owner truth.
Mounted workload IPC is internal transport and does not become a public capability claim.
Every required-now PG gate remains owned and open in the production-gate ledger.
The gate becomes blocking only when its named conditional posture is selected.
The gate belongs to a later horizon and is not closed by M0.
The selected profile excludes this deployment, commercial, legal, or physical posture.
No qualifying end-to-end selected-profile product cohort exists as of the baseline date.
No terminal selected-profile runtime and fault cohort exists as of the baseline date.
No invoice-reconciled selected-profile run cohort exists as of the baseline date.
No qualifying first-time-operator comprehension cohort exists as of the baseline date.

bounded_discovery_exclusions.note: Dynamic raw bytes cannot be enumerated; the lease-gated wildcard proxy entry is censused and this implementation is file-locked.
Encrypted dynamic frames cannot be enumerated here; the internal wildcard channel entry and typed public/workload RPC registries are censused.
Harvested/generated compatibility bundle; active owned adapter and outbound crossings are censused instead of treating bundle code as authority.
Dynamic inbound compatibility dispatch and augmentation loading are file-locked while every statically visible outbound crossing is enumerated.
The spawned reference server has dynamic fixture, missing-chunk, and static-file fallbacks; its single ANY compatibility boundary is censused and the whole dispatch is file-locked.

discovery_source_coverage.rule: A new production route registry, RPC registry, service implementation, listener source, active JavaScript effect source, or JavaScript filesystem/process action fails before its entries can be consumed.

m0_exit_policy.claim_scope: M0 program control and claim lock only
required_conditions: every discovered entry is explicitly reviewed and source-anchored
every selected object has an owner
every selected effect has a verified final invoker or explicit unavailable blocker
all 58 PG ids are mapped exactly once without closure claims
every baseline and evidence item is closed or honestly named

entries.selected_profile_rationale: The selected profile does not activate every program-integrity and selected upgrade posture; this gate blocks if its named posture is selected.
The selected single-node software-change profile directly needs program-integrity and selected upgrade; the gate remains open.

Portable delegated authority is required only for the managed-optionality overlay. The sovereign-local lane uses locally permitted nonportable authority and makes no portable-authority claim.
Every selected consequential local or managed policy-enforcement point must verify its lane's current authority before the final invocation; copied authority fields are insufficient.
The selected sovereign-local journey requires ordinary and consequential receipts through Agentgres-owned append, scheduled checkpoints, and crash-safe inclusion and consistency evidence.

The selected local export must verify offline integrity and valid-as-of posture from declared signer, key, revocation, and temporal inputs without confusing authentic historical proof with current authority.

release_ladder.criterion: One fixture traverses selected owner boundaries; it is not production-ready.
release_ladder.name: walking skeleton

release_ladder.criterion: The terminal M9 journey works with adversarial evidence in an IOI-controlled environment.
release_ladder.name: internal product proof

release_ladder.criterion: Independent users complete the journey on their data with disclosed support and limits.
release_ladder.name: external design-partner proof

release_ladder.criterion: Applicable required-now and selected conditional gates close and recovery thresholds pass.
release_ladder.name: production-integrated profile

release_ladder.criterion: One logical System preserves continuity and performs useful work across admitted nodes.
release_ladder.name: distributed L0 proof

release_ladder.criterion: Independently governed Systems demonstrate useful positive-surplus work and portable exit.
release_ladder.name: sovereign cooperation proof

release_ladder.criterion: A selected live hardware profile passes its physical-action, runtime, and production gates.
release_ladder.name: embodied deployment proof

release_ladder.criterion: Connected and secured services show recurring external demand and sustainable security economics.
release_ladder.name: public network demand proof

repository_validation_baselines.nonclaim: This records repository-wide pre-existing formatting debt; M0 changes no Rust source and claims no Rustfmt closure.

exact_effect.effect: Apply or refuse one sandboxed repository software change.

included: one immutable package and release through genesis into one stable System
one immutable GoalRunProfile and durable GoalRun in an OutcomeRoom
disclosed first-party worker roles plus an independent deterministic verifier
one isolated sandbox repository and branch
deployment-local identity and a low-risk local product session with locally permitted authority, plus an optional provider-neutral connected overlay with passkey-capable portable authority
exact authority, revocation, IFC, budget, fence, idempotency, recovery, and receipt checks
Agentgres operations, heads, roots, receipts, restart, replay, backup, restore, evidence admission, export, and offline verification
private evaluation and model routes with measured internal cost only
proposal-mediated improvement with target-owner activation, rollback or recall, suspension, and retirement
selected_profile.level: minimum selected L0 proof profile; not a MinimumL0 object
nonclaims: no multiple runtime nodes, automatic failover, or cross-system AIIP
no public marketplace, payment, payout, settlement, IOI Network, IOI L1, or native asset
no physical actuation, cTEE claim, public certification, or generalized recursive improvement
no autonomous production access, universal correctness, universal factual truth, or hidden provider non-learning
minimum L0 proves only the sovereign-local lane; managed optionality remains unclaimed until its ordered connected overlay passes
no architecture or production capability closes merely because M0 program control verifies

object_owners.object_set: System package, release, genesis, constitution, deployment, membership, and lifecycle
GoalRunProfile, GoalRun, GoalGroundingLoop, ContextCell, and handoff
OutcomeRoom, participation, frontier, claim, attempt, finding, challenge, WorkResult, and OutcomeDelta
Worker topology, harness invocation, scoped tools, model routes, and selected repository effect
Deployment-local identity, product access, account and entitlement boundaries, and metering posture
Deployment-local policy and locally permitted exact-effect authority provider selection
object_owners.owner: Local/domain governance and the selected authority provider

object_owners.object_set: Connected product-session binding, portable approval and grant, revocation, and step-up
Operation, exact head/root, receipt, replay, checkpoint, artifact lineage, and export
Provider route and isolated work environment
Proposal-mediated improvement, target approval, activation, rollback, recall, and suspension
Learning eligibility, source rights, route rights, export, and revocation impact
Visible product projections and unavailable/degraded/completed states

proof_lane_baseline_extension.cohort: Fresh standalone deployments complete the sovereign-local lane with every IOI-managed endpoint denied; the same Systems enter the connected lane only for managed-optionality claims.
proof_lane_baseline_extension.rule: Additive proof-lane target overlay only; it does not rewrite the frozen 2026-07-18 M0 baselines.

selected_profile.proof_lane_validation_posture: semantically validated exact lane ids, order, prerequisites, evidence sets, blocker identities, route sets, and canon-anchored owner sources; named blockers remain open until the product journeys pass

proof_lanes.authority_posture: deployment-local authentication remains distinct from locally permitted exact-effect authority; no portable delegated-authority claim
proof_lanes.claim_requirement: required for the minimum-L0 local-completeness claim and every stronger claim that depends on it
proof_lanes.endpoint_posture: all IOI-managed endpoints denied; loopback and declared local IPC allowed
required_evidence: offline evidence export and independent verification

proof_lanes.authority_posture: provider-neutral account authentication plus passkey-capable context-bound portable authority when connected policy requires it
proof_lanes.claim_requirement: required only for managed-optionality claims and claims that depend on managed attachment
proof_lanes.connection_posture: explicitly attach one named managed service, execute one admitted operation, prove no implicit transfer or unnamed-use billing, then revoke or detach it
required_evidence: connected identity and context-bound portable authority when required
one explicitly leased and receipted named managed-service use
exact binding, data-view, lease, RuntimeAssignment, custody, usage, charge, and receipt inspection
zero implicit transfer or charge without named service use
post-detach continuation for locally satisfied dependencies
proof_lanes.starting_state: the same independently operable System after a passing sovereign-local lane

rollback_stop_rules: Stop on any hidden database edit, privileged one-off script, copied bearer authority, prompt-only transition, fabricated success, or manually reconstructed evidence chain.
An unavailable owner step remains a typed blocker; compatibility output never substitutes for it.
Revoke or fence authority before recovery, preserve ambiguous effects, and require explicit reconciliation before retry.
Stop on any silent truth, authority, custody, writer, execution, or billing transfer, or when detachment breaks work whose complete dependency closure remains locally satisfied.

visible_terminal_journey.visible_action: Authenticate through deployment-local identity with every IOI-managed endpoint denied; in the ordered managed overlay, begin from the same independently operable System, link an eligible provider-neutral account, and explicitly enable one named managed service.
Describe the goal, repository, constraints, authority, and acceptance.
Validate, preview, and simulate one compiled package/genesis proposal.
Approve genesis and inspect the stable System.
Start or admit the GoalRun in its OutcomeRoom-backed work context.
Observe planning, claimed work, attempts, verification, and blockers.
Review the exact proposed repository effect.

Satisfy the lane's exact scoped authority ceremony: locally permitted nonportable authority in the sovereign-local lane, and context-bound portable authority with passkey step-up only when connected policy requires it.
Let the daemon revalidate and execute or refuse the exact effect.

Inspect the diff, tests, evidence admission, receipt chain, state root, costs, provider route, and learning eligibility; in the managed overlay also inspect the named service's exact bindings, data views, leases, RuntimeAssignment, custody, usage, charges, and receipts.
Restart and replay the decision and effect; back up, restore, export, and independently verify the evidence offline.
In the managed overlay, revoke or disconnect the named service and prove locally satisfiable continuation; in both lanes, propose an upgrade, exercise rollback or recall, and retire the System.

sequencing_authority.rule: External untracked operator inputs may sequence work, but only tracked canon and conformance sources bound in canon_basis provide committed M0 evidence.

tracked_architecture_evidence_authority.role: committed architecture and status evidence authority
